title: arenaria peploides subsp. major
intro: Arenaria peploides subsp. major, also known as sea sandwort, is a species of flowering plant in the carnation family that is commonly found in coastal areas and salt-sprayed regions. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that may help to soothe skin irritations and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of Arenaria peploides subsp. major include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and provide protection against oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of this plant are often used in topical applications, such as creams and salves, to take advantage of its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
